front: Painting: Monochrome self-portrait with a cigarette in his mouth
Painting: Divided portraits into grids of small squares filled with concentric circles/rings of color that resolve into faces from a distance
Painting: Portrait of Bill Clinton using grid-like divisions; Guerrilla Girls offered three potential wall labels for it
Painting: Mosaic portraits at 86th Street subway station in New York, including ones of Kara Walker, Lou Reed, and Philip Glass
Painting: Photorealistic portrait of a bespectacled man in a plaid shirt; hangs in MoMA
Artist: Became paralyzed from the neck down after a seizure/spinal artery collapse in 1988; called it "The Event"
Artist: Strapped a paintbrush to his arm/wrist to continue painting after paralysis
Artist: Part of the photorealism / hyperrealism movement, alongside Richard Estes, Audrey Flack, and Ralph Goings
Artist: Sometimes also grouped with Minimalism
Artist: Called Cindy Sherman "perhaps the most important artist" of the 1980s; painted her in a multicolored shawl
Artist: Collaborated with daguerreotypist Jerry Spagnoli on works such as Untitled Torso
Artist: An early abstract painting may have been rediscovered by this artist's lawyer's dog walker in 2022
Painting: Housed in the Walker Art Gallery
